provenance.source_corpus: Biotic and Abiotic Effects of Remnant and Restoration Soils on the Performance of Tallgrass Prairie Species

source_spans.text: The higher phosphate level in the restoration sites was likely a result of the past level of human nutrient addition and disturbance on these sites.
The higher phosphate level in the restoration sites was likely a result of the past level of human nutrient addition and disturbance on these sites.
Soil from restored sites was more phosphorus rich, promoting greater growth of both a common and a rare prairie species when grown in intact soil cores.
Soil from restored sites was more phosphorus rich, promoting greater growth of both a common and a rare prairie species when grown in intact soil cores.
Our results suggest that plants on restored prairies are less reliant on soil microbes due to the higher fertility found within their soils.
Our results suggest that plants on restored prairies are less reliant on soil microbes due to the higher fertility found within their soils.
Our data suggests that like other ecosystems, residual high fertility in tallgrass prairies may facilitate invasion by non-native plants.
Our data suggests that like other ecosystems, residual high fertility in tallgrass prairies may facilitate invasion by non-native plants.
In the short term disturbances create conditions both for the establishment of exotic species and weedy species seedlings.

nodes.name: establishment of exotic species and weedy species seedlings

source_spans.text: In the short term disturbances create conditions both for the establishment of exotic species and weedy species seedlings.
In the long term they can alter the competitive balance between native and exotics when increased soil fertility lessens the advantage native species gain through interactions with soil microbes
In the long term they can alter the competitive balance between native and exotics when increased soil fertility lessens the advantage native species gain through interactions with soil microbes

nodes.name: addition of organic matter to soil

source_spans.text: For instance, the addition of organic matter to soil can dramatically lower soil nitrate levels and decrease the abundance of exotics in restored grasslands
For instance, the addition of organic matter to soil can dramatically lower soil nitrate levels and decrease the abundance of exotics in restored grasslands
For instance, the addition of organic matter to soil can dramatically lower soil nitrate levels and decrease the abundance of exotics in restored grasslands
Plants grown on inoculated soil had significantly greater growth than plants grown on sterilized soil
Plants grown on inoculated soil had significantly greater growth than plants grown on sterilized soil

Additionally, plants from prairie remnants were more reliant on the soil biota for their growth, whereas plants from restored sites tended to be unaffected by the presence of soil microbes in the soil.
demonstrating that plants benefit more from soil biota when the soil is less fertile.
demonstrating that plants benefit more from soil biota when the soil is less fertile.
